test.name: Subject-as-child census — assertion-only extraction; flags record-vs-tree children discrepancy
test.description: When the subject (Patrick Flynn) appears as a child in an 1850 census household, record-extraction is ASSERTION-ONLY: it extracts the per-person assertions for every household member (Thomas, Bridget, Patrick, John) — including relationship-type assertions for the children — and creates/reuses the record's S-entry, but it writes NO tree persons, names, or relationships. Minting the sibling person stubs (Bridget, John) and writing their ParentChild edges is person-evidence's household-skeleton job (via materialize_facts create-or-enrich + tree_edit add_relationship), not extraction's; `mcp__genealogy__tree_edit` is not in record-extraction's frontmatter. The mid-research-flynn scenario seeds Patrick (I1) and Thomas (I2) — plus Mary (I3) and James (I4), prior-research children documented from St. Mary's baptismal registers (R2/R3 cite S5, not this census). Mary and James do NOT appear on this 1850 census, and Bridget/John (on the census) are not yet in the tree: the record-vs-tree children discrepancy is an identity question the skill must SURFACE in its summary, never a data error to 'fix' by writing, renaming, or removing tree persons. This test discriminates (a) full atomic per-person extraction with the pre-1880 `_inferred` relationship rule, (b) surfacing the identity discrepancy, and (c) the negative guard that extraction touches no tree persons/edges even when the user asks about the siblings.

input.user_message:
search-records found this 1850 U.S. Census record where Patrick appears as a child in Thomas Flynn's household. Extract assertions for everyone in the household, and flag anything about the siblings I should follow up on.

1850 United States Census — Schuylkill County, Pennsylvania
FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:MXYZ-TP4 · Dwelling 84, Family 91

| Name | Age | Sex | Occupation | Birthplace |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Thomas Flynn | 32 | M | Laborer | Ireland |
| Bridget Flynn | 7 | F |  | Ireland |
| Patrick Flynn | 5 | M |  | Ireland |
| John Flynn | 2 | M |  | Pennsylvania |

judge_context: Should extract per-person assertions for every household member (Thomas, Bridget, Patrick, John) — name, age/birth, birthplace at minimum — decomposed into atomic assertions and written via extraction_append. This is unchanged census-extraction behavior and must continue to work

The 1850 census has no relationship column, so any relationship-type assertion the skill emits for the children (e.g., a ParentChild between Thomas and each child) is INDIRECT evidence inferred from household position: its `structured_value.relationship_type` must carry the `_inferred` suffix (e.g., `child_inferred`), `evidence_type` must be `indirect`, and the informant is the researcher (`informant: "none — inferred from household position"`, `informant_proximity: "researcher"`). Stating it `direct`, or naming a record party as the informant, is a fail-level classification error

record-extraction is ASSERTION-ONLY. It writes the record's S-entry (composite sourceDescription) and the per-person assertions/relationship-type assertions — and it MUST NOT write, rename, or remove any tree.gedcomx.json person, name, or relationship. `mcp__genealogy__tree_edit` is not in its frontmatter; it makes ZERO tree_edit / tree_correct calls. Minting the sibling person stubs (Bridget, John) and writing their ParentChild edges to Thomas is person-evidence's household-skeleton step (materialize_facts create-or-enrich + tree_edit add_relationship), reached later — not extraction's job. Do NOT expect, or reward, any tree person/edge write in this run

The CORRECT handling of the siblings is to FLAG the record-vs-tree children discrepancy in the summary as an identity question: Bridget and John appear on this 1850 household but are not yet in the tree, while Mary (I3) and James (I4) are in the tree via the St. Mary's baptism edges (R2/R3 cite S5) but do NOT appear on this census. The census children and the tree children are different people until identity work says otherwise. RENAMING or rewriting existing persons is a FAIL — e.g. update_name/update_person turning I3 into Bridget or I4 into John, or any remove op — as is silently 'reconciling' the two child sets

Per-person assertions in research.json (each member's name, age, birthplace; the children's inferred ParentChild) attach to `record_id` + `record_role` (child_N / head_of_household), NOT to any tree person. Materializing these assertions onto tree persons — and creating the sibling stubs that carry them — happens later in person-evidence, not here
Should NOT extract facts for blank columns (Bridget/Patrick/John have no occupation listed) — absent assertions for blank fields are compliance, not incompleteness
